items.title: Two people die from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking NT health alert

items.title: Randwick mayor defends parking meter plan for popular eastern Sydney beaches

items.title: Eligibility expanded for Queensland's free nasal spray flu vaccine program
items.summary: With stocks set to expire in August, the FluMist nasal spray has been made free for all children over the age of two.

items.title: Brief of evidence delays for Pheobe Bishop murder-accused Tanika Bromley
items.summary: The defence team for Tanika Bromley, who is charged over the murder of Queensland teenager Pheobe Bishop, raises issues about delays in receiving parts of the brief of evidence.

items.title: Key takeaways from Sam Altman and Elon Musk's bitter legal battle over OpenAI
items.summary: A legal showdown between two of the tech world's most powerful men has reached its end. Here's what we learned throughout the trial.

items.title: Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions gets $18.9 million in funding after sounding alarm over rising caseload
items.summary: The funding will go towards 24 new, permanent positions within the office to meet its increasing case load, Attorney-General Tara Cheyne said.

items.title: What we know about the deadly shooting at a San Diego mosque

items.title: Network Ten to go off air in country SA and NSW as WIN Television deal lapses
items.summary: The end of a broadcast deal between Network Ten and Win Television is set to leave three regional markets without access to multiple channels.

items.title: Kalman Tal paid John Yalu $5,000 cash to perform his fatal amputation, court hears
items.summary: Kalman Tal, 66, died soon after he asked for his left foot to be cut off in a public park with a battery-powered saw, a court has heard, but the man who performed the amputation has pleaded not guilty to murder.

items.title: Woman charged over death of 79yo man after alleged Vincentia 'road rage' assault

items.title: SA Power Networks' $10 million plan to combat insulator pollution linked to regional summer power outages
items.summary: SA Power Networks is investing about $10 million to combat insulator pollution, which has been linked to repeated power outages across regional South Australia over summer.

items.title: Scientists investigate mystery illness paralysing and killing hundreds of magpies
items.summary: Researchers say they are still far from identifying the cause of a deadly paralytic illness spreading through populations of the iconic Australian birds.

items.title: Tasmania government apologises for display of autopsy body parts in museum without consent

items.title: 'Nowhere for me to exist': Growing up as a biracial child in Australia

items.title: NSW Police Association pushes for night courts to ease pressure on frontline officers

items.title: Dave Hughes on family, sobriety and creating laughter until his dying day
items.summary: Comedian Dave Hughes spoke to The Assembly about why he quit alcohol at 22, being "addicted to work" and the type of dad he's trying to be for his three kids.

items.title: This week in history quiz, May 18: Where was Pac-Man first released?
items.summary: In what year did John Howard become a federal MP? And which country got to play Pac-Man first? Test yourself in this week's history quiz.

items.title: Train driver charged after deadly crash in Bangkok
items.summary: Thai police have charged a train driver after a crash on Saturday in central Bangkok in which a freight train collided with a public bus, killing eight people.

items.title: Six stores ordered to shut as WA's illegal tobacco laws come into force
items.summary: More than 140,000 illegal cigarettes have been seized and six stores ordered to close down, days after Western Australia's new tobacco laws came into force.

items.title: Chalmers' budget targets housing affordability, but misses supply
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Perth father Steven Mattaboni identified as Rottnest shark attack victim
items.summary: The wife of 38-year-old Perth man Steven Mattaboni says her family's "hearts are irrevocably broken" after he was killed in a shark attack while spearfishing off the coast of Rottnest Island on Saturday.

items.title: Blues suffer Origin blow, Ponga will 'play anywhere' for Maroons
items.summary: NSW has been dealt a State of Origin blow ahead of the Blues' team announcement on Monday, with Latrell Mitchell ruling himself out of Game I selection.

items.title: Harley heroics lift Eagles to stunning upset win over Giants
items.summary: West Coast surges to a third victory of the season, defeating GWS by 17 points while St Kilda and Fremantle also enjoy Sunday wins.

items.title: Woman who had been dead for weeks found at New Town house
items.summary: The body of a woman who had been dead for weeks has been found at a house in the Hobart suburb of New Town.

items.title: Love story: How an Aussie rocker and a punk grandma found harmony
items.summary: Singer/guitarist Mick Medew and self-proclaimed punk grandma Ursula Collie were in their mid-50s when they met and bonded over a mutual love of live music. A decade on, they're musical and life partners.

items.title: Teenage pedestrian dies after being hit in Bentley shopping centre car park
items.summary: A 19-year-old driver is being questioned by police after a teenager is struck by a car in a Perth shopping centre car park and dies in hospital.

items.title: Labor claims victory in Stafford by-election in Brisbane, LNP concedes candidate 'just won't get there'
items.summary: Labor candidate Luke Richmond claims victory over the LNP's Fiona Hammond in a tight race in the north Brisbane electorate of Stafford.

items.title: Police reopen cold case into armed robbery at Chadstone Shopping Centre
items.summary: Police are appealing for help to find the man believed to be behind a 1994 armed robbery at a Chadstone cinema that left three other men with gunshot wounds.

items.title: Jim Chalmers defends impact of tax changes on young investors
items.summary: Jim Chalmers says it is better for young people to invest based on economic rather than tax outcomes, and argued they can still access negative gearing by buying up new homes.

items.title: Reef restoration brings back marine life and boosts Swan River's health
items.summary: Work is underway to restore Perth's Swan River and help its severely degraded riverbed, where 1 million tonnes of limestone was taken in the 1990s.

items.title: Western Australia's first fainting goats settle into farm near Busselton
items.summary: The goats, which "faint" when stressed or excited, have travelled to a farm near Busselton, where they will be bred as pets.

items.title: Man charged with murder after alleged assault north of Bundaberg
items.summary: The 33-year-old Bundaberg North man was refused police bail after being charged with murder.

items.title: Eurovision key moments: from Euro-pop to Euro-slop as Delta Goodrem makes Australia proud
items.summary: Slept through Eurovision this year? Here's what you missed from Europe's favourite song contest, with boycotts, ballads and a Bulgarian song called Bangaranga making headlines.

items.title: Sydney lawyer was stressed out by her mortgage so she sold up and bought a van
items.summary: Phoebe Macdougall says she hated living with a mortgage, so she is trying van life instead.

items.title: Manchester City wins FA Cup final over Chelsea, Pep Guardiola maintains Premier League trophy focus
items.summary: Manchester City's FA Cup celebrations after a 1-0 win over Chelsea will be low key, as the team refocuses on its tight Premier League title race with Arsenal.

items.title: First photo of wild Myrmeleon houstoni taken by unsuspecting Mildura woman on bushwalk
items.summary: There were no known photographs taken in the wild of the Myrmeleon houstoni until a regional Victorian woman snapped a photo of one while on a bushwalk.

items.title: Canberra family creates foundation for youth sports to honour daughter killed in bus crash
items.summary: Uqasha Imran was fatally injured when a bus driver struck her on her motorcycle in 2025. Now, her family are promoting the importance of organ donation while helping other young athletes like her.

items.title: Gold Coast e-bike crackdown 'not effective', council report finds
items.summary: A Gold Coast council report finds an e-bike crackdown has failed to significantly reduce anti-social behaviour, with many riders ignoring compliance officers.

items.title: Tasmanian town of Ross working to be accredited by Dark Sky International
items.summary: The northern Tasmanian town of Ross is working to protect its night sky, a years-long process that would see it formally recognised for its darkness and clarity.

items.title: Rider, Stacey Smith completes a full run at the Topar campdraft in April this year.

items.title: Topar Campdraft Clinic riders take the reins and get hooked on the sport
items.summary: Amid the red dirt and flies of an outback stockyard, thrillseekers with a passion for horses learn about a quintessential Aussie sport.

items.title: APY Art Centre Collective calls for federal inquiry into First Nations art industry
items.summary: APY Art Centre Collective members say a voluntary code of conduct isn't protecting artists from financial exploitation and misconduct in the industry.

items.title: Could wine provide Australia's next source of biofuel?
items.summary: Australia's wine industry is investigating whether its current 263 million litre glut of wine would be better used as biofuel to power cars, trucks or even aircraft.

items.title: Councils hit by millions in copper theft damage across Queensland infrastructure, leaving roads in darkness
items.summary: The practice is costing Queensland ratepayers millions of dollars in repairs, and leading to widespread outages to lighting, communications and community sporting facilities.

items.title: Victoria's political donation system remains a free-for-all ahead of election
items.summary: With only six months before the state election, the Victorian government struggles to devise new donation laws after the High Court found previous laws unconstitutional.

items.title: Gurrumul’s ARIA Hall of Fame honour brings pride and reflection for family
items.summary: Nearly a decade since his death, Gurrumul is set to be inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame, bringing pride and reflection to a remote part of Australia.

items.title: First home buyers buoyed by negative gearing, capital gains changes at first Saturday auctions since budget
items.summary: The government says the changes are aimed at levelling the playing field for young buyers bidding against property investors, while the opposition is pledging to undo the reforms, citing their potential to raise rents.

items.title: NSW government funds Aboriginal-led south coast sea urchin enterprise with $1.48m grant
items.summary: After decades of fighting for their traditional fishing rights, Aboriginal custodians are now fighting to save their sea country from ecological collapse.

items.title: Sam Kerr ties Chelsea's goals record in final Women's Super League match
items.summary: Sam Kerr ties the record for the most Women's Super League goals for Chelsea as she plays her final match with the London club after more than six years.

items.title: Rewiring Australia campaign for renters to be included in electrical future
items.summary: A standard power plug for charging your home from an EV is among the measures proposed to improve energy standards for rental properties.

items.title: Malnourished and overtrained, Olympic gymnast didn't have energy for a period
items.summary: Alexandra Kiroi-Bogatyreva trained up to 10 hours a day, six days a week, often without the proper nutrition. She wants others to know what that can do to the body.

items.title: Aspiration has changed since the Howard era. This budget is finally catching up
items.summary: At the turn of the century, "aspiration" described Australians who wanted to put their kids into private school and university, and to build their wealth by getting an investment property or two. Today, it means something humbler; millions of younger Australians simply aspire to own a home one day, to have some shelter.

items.title: 'Accidental leader' of CFA levy protest Rob Armstrong reveals scars left by childhood bushfire trauma
items.summary: Every time farmer and volunteer firefighter Rob Armstrong gets a call to battle a bushfire, something inside says not to do it. But he goes anyway. He knows what it's like to be trapped in the flames and rescued by volunteers.

items.title: What do governments need to do this time to avoid pre-NDIS mistakes?
items.summary: The states are once again on the hook for disability services due to the Commonwealth's drastic NDIS cuts. Can the mistakes of the past be avoided?

items.title: The spiral-shaped trap: AI chatbots and the descent into delusion
items.summary: Rodrigues thought an AI chatbot could help him escape poverty and build wealth. Instead, he became trapped in a spiral of delusion and dependence.

items.title: Khaled Sabsabi's new installations 'didn't have a home'. Now they're at the Venice Biennale
items.summary: Last year, Khaled Sabsabi was appointed, sacked and then reinstated as Australia's representative to the Venice Biennale. Now he opens two installations at the world's most prestigious art exhibition.

items.title: Rochelle Jordan on Through The Wall, critical acclaim and learning to love her own music
items.summary: The Canadian singer won widespread acclaim with her third album. Now she brings it to Australia.

items.title: Can you score top marks on Guy Montgomery's spelling quiz?
items.summary: Are you ready to test your spelling skills like your little lunch depends on it? Come on in.

items.title: Sydney FC defeats Newcastle Jets on penalties to progress to A-League Men grand final
items.summary: Eli Adams's 96th-minute equaliser proves to be in vain as Sydney FC downs Newcastle on penalties to progress to the A-League Men season decider.

items.title: Labor's Luke Richmond on track to win by-election in Brisbane seat of Stafford
items.summary: At the end of counting on Saturday night, there had been a 4.1 per cent swing against Labor after preferences, with additional postal votes still to be counted.

items.title: Melbourne City wins A-League Women's championship as Matildas star Holly McNamara shines against Wellington Phoenix
items.summary: Sensational strikes from young stars Holly McNamara and Leticia McKenna have helped Melbourne City to a record-equalling fifth A-League Women championship.

items.title: Virat Kohli is ready to play for India at 2027 World Cup, but only if he adds something to the team
items.summary: Virat Kohli says if he plays in the 2027 ICC World Cup he will compete for "every ball like it's the last ball" - but vows if he can't add value to India's team he will not be there.

items.title: Tasmania to introduce new vicarious liability laws, child sex abuse survivor and advocate welcomes move
items.summary: Tasmania will introduce laws to close a loophole which allows some organisations to avoid being liable for abuse perpetrated by individuals who aren't formal employees.

items.title: Origin hopefuls stand up as Cowboys coach hails 'gritty' win
items.summary: Todd Payten said his side's victory over the Roosters was "as good a win as I've had" with the club as Melbourne notched back-to-back wins after a messy 34-8 victory over Parramatta and the Sea Eagles thrash Wests Tigers in Magic Round at Lang Park.

items.title: How Harvey Weinstein's fourth trial placed #MeToo's legacy on the stand
items.summary: Almost a decade after the #MeToo movement began, disgraced producer Harvey Weinstein's latest trial took place in a new climate. But supporters says its significance remains.

items.title: How Narendra Modi's government is shaping India into a one-party state
items.summary: How Prime Minister Narendra Modi is turning India into a one-party nation.

items.title: Man stabbed in his sleep and left in serious condition in Tennant Creek, NT police say
items.summary: A 23-year-old man has been arrested over the alleged stabbing of another man who was sleeping in a home in remote Tennant Creek about 990 kilometres south-east of Darwin.

items.title: Scottish FA condemns 'vigilantism' after referee's details leak online for controversial penalty
items.summary: Scottish FA condemns "vigilantism" after a referee's personal details are leaked online following a highly controversial penalty in the Scottish Premier League.

items.title: Water Bank project designed to drought-proof Australia's biggest outback city misses out in federal budget
items.summary: A project designed to drought-proof Australia's biggest outback city has missed out on funding in this week's federal budget, with the City of Kalgoorlie-Boulder set to review its ambitious plans for what has been dubbed Water Bank.

items.title: Blues claim spirited victory over Dogs to begin post-Voss life in style
items.summary: Carlton beats the Western Bulldogs by 12 points in its first game since Michael Voss left the club, while Melbourne upsets Hawthorn in style. Follow live.

items.title: Man dead after Rottnest Island shark attack
items.summary: A 38-year-old man had been spearfishing with friends when he was killed in a shark attack off Rottnest Island on Saturday morning.

items.title: Where to watch Eurovision in Australia, how to vote and what to know about Delta Goodrem's song
items.summary: The Eurovision Song Contest is on this Sunday, which will mean an early start for superfans of the pop culture phenomenon.

items.title: A hearty slow-cooked beef stew that will warm you up as the weather cools - ABC News
items.summary: Just 15 minutes of prep means your slow cooker will have a rich beef stew waiting at dinner time.

items.title: Farmers in limbo as $100m in Inland Rail land sits idle
items.summary: For more than a decade, Australians in the path of the proposed Inland Rail corridor have wondered if the nation-building project will force them from their homes. Although the northern section has been scrapped, the legacy of its blueprints is still fuelling anxiety.

items.title: Bill to support environmental water flows an 'attack' on property rights
items.summary: Politicians and landholders vow to fight a controversial new New South Wales law that will allow the inundation of private property from environmental water flows under the Murray-Darling Basin Plan.

items.title: No word on extending excise cut as Anthony Albanese says fuel supply above pre-war levels
items.summary: The prime minister has left the door open to extending the fuel excise cut beyond June, while reporting Australia's fuel supply is now above pre-Iran war levels.

items.title: Residents fear Oyster Bay shallowing out and becoming mudflat
items.summary: A bin that has been sticking out of the mud for 12 years has become a symbol for an ongoing issue of sediment build-up in Oyster Bay in Sydney's Sutherland Shire.

items.title: Israel targets Hamas leader in air strikes in Gaza killing seven Palestinians
items.summary: Seven Palestinians, including a child, have died in Friday's air strikes, which Israel said targeted Hamas militant leader Izz al-Din al-Haddad.

items.title: Passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrive in Perth

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship Australians isolating in Perth unlikely to be paid out, experts say
items.summary: But the lawyer who led the class action in the COVID-19 era Ruby Princess case has warned the cruise company will likely argue Australian law had no jurisdiction over the matter.

items.title: First Nations father teaches dying Indigenous language to sons through bedtime story
items.summary: Five years ago, Gudjala man Braithen Knox began learning his mob's language, which has fewer than 10 speakers. Now a father of two, he is passing it on to his sons through bedtime stories.
